title: NOAA Coral Reef Watch 5km BAA-max & DHW-max Sample Monthly Rasters
abstract: Two standalone monthly NOAA Coral Reef Watch (CRW) 5km satellite coral bleaching heat stress rasters found loose in the Environmental folder root: a Bleaching Alert Area maximum (BAA-max) composite for April 1985 and a Degree Heating Week maximum (DHW-max) composite for January 2023. These appear to be single-month sample/test downloads of the standard CRW v3.1 product suite rather than a continuous archived time series.

variables.name: bleaching_alert_area
variables.label: Bleaching Alert Area (max)
variables.description: Monthly maximum Bleaching Alert Area value from the NOAA CRW v3.1 global 5km product suite, indicating peak coral bleaching heat stress alert level for the month.

variables.name: degree_heating_week
variables.label: Degree Heating Week (max)
variables.description: Monthly maximum Degree Heating Week value from the NOAA CRW v3.1 global 5km product suite, accumulated thermal stress over a trailing 12-week window.

steward.name: Unassigned - KAUST Red Sea Data Team

access.how_to_request: Publicly downloadable from NOAA Coral Reef Watch (coralreefwatch.noaa.gov); no approval needed.

provenance.citation: NOAA Coral Reef Watch. 2026. CoralTemp Version 3.1, Daily Global 5km Satellite Coral Bleaching Heat Stress Monitoring Products.

known_issues: Global (not Red-Sea-clipped) rasters; bbox recorded as global extent since no Red-Sea-specific subsetting was evident in the filename or found alongside it.
Only two isolated months present (1985-04 and 2023-01), not a continuous series - likely sample/test downloads rather than a maintained archive.
Variable names (bleaching_alert_area, degree_heating_week) inferred from standard CRW filename conventions ('baa-max', 'dhw-max'), not verified by opening the files.
